qa_pairs.question: Who maintains the Subatomic and High Energy Physics Software page?
qa_pairs.answer: The Compute Canada Subatomic Physics National Team maintains this page.

qa_pairs.question: When was the Subatomic and High Energy Physics Software page last updated?
qa_pairs.answer: The Subatomic and High Energy Physics Software page was last updated in July 2021.

qa_pairs.question: Is there a reference page available for the Astronomy and High Energy Physics Interactive Analysis Facility?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, there is a reference page for the Astronomy and High Energy Physics Interactive Analysis Facility.

qa_pairs.question: What do many subatomic experimental physics groups rely on for software?
qa_pairs.answer: Many subatomic experimental physics groups rely on CVMFS repositories from CERN or the Open Science Grid, and specific repositories for each experiment.

qa_pairs.question: Why might the CCenv setup for regular users create conflicts with other repositories?
qa_pairs.answer: The CCenv setup for regular users might create conflicts because standard libraries are provided from the Compute Canada soft.computecanada.ca CVMFS repository, which uses Nix and Easybuild for software access, rather than having software installed on the base OS of compute nodes.

qa_pairs.question: Where can ATLAS users find helpful information regarding Compute Canada Tier-3s and Containers?
qa_pairs.answer: ATLAS users can find helpful pages on the TRIUMF twiki at https://twiki.atlas-canada.ca/bin/view/AtlasCanada/ComputeCanadaTier3s and https://twiki.atlas-canada.ca/bin/view/AtlasCanada/Containers.

qa_pairs.question: What is the recommendation for ATLAS users regarding Tier-3 setups?
qa_pairs.answer: ATLAS users should use the recommended setups for Tier-3 use rather than reinventing techniques described in the document.

qa_pairs.question: Are HEP_OSLibs packages installed on Compute Canada (CC) computing nodes?
qa_pairs.answer: No, HEP_OSLibs packages/rpms are not installed on the Compute Canada computing nodes, although many setups assume they are.

qa_pairs.question: What is the suggested approach for setups that require RPMs not present on Compute Canada nodes?
qa_pairs.answer: The suggested approach is to use singularity containers, which have the necessary RPMs installed.

qa_pairs.question: What additional benefit do singularity containers offer on CentOS7-based Compute Canada infrastructure?
qa_pairs.answer: Singularity containers allow the use of different OS bases, such as SL6, on the CentOS7-based Compute Canada infrastructure.

qa_pairs.question: How can a CentOS7-based view with gcc8 be set up from sft.cern.ch?
qa_pairs.answer: To set up a CentOS7-based view with gcc8 from sft.cern.ch, one can use the command: `source /cvmfs/sft.cern.ch/lcg/views/setupViews.sh LCG_95 x86_64-centos7-gcc8-opt`.

qa_pairs.question: What does setting up a CentOS7-based view from sft.cern.ch using LCG_95 x86_64-centos7-gcc8-opt include?
qa_pairs.answer: This setup will include the necessary paths to compilers, Geant4, ROOT, and other components.

qa_pairs.question: What are some of the available <arch-os-compiler> setups for LCG_95?
qa_pairs.answer: Some available setups for LCG_95 include `x86_64-centos7-gcc7-dbg`, `x86_64-centos7-gcc7-opt`, `x86_64-centos7-gcc8-dbg`, `x86_64-centos7-gcc8-opt`, `x86_64-slc6-gcc62-opt`, `x86_64-slc6-gcc7-dbg`, `x86_64-slc6-gcc7-opt`, `x86_64-slc6-gcc8-dbg`, `x86_64-slc6-gcc8-opt`, `x86_64-ubuntu1804-gcc7-opt`, `x86_64-ubuntu1804-gcc8-dbg`, and `x86_64-ubuntu1804-gcc8-opt`.

qa_pairs.question: Where can a list of all RPMs installed via HEPOS_Libs for CentOS7 be found?
qa_pairs.answer: A list of all RPMs installed via HEPOS_Libs for CentOS7 is available at https://gitlab.cern.ch/linuxsupport/rpms/HEP_OSlibs/blob/7.2.11-3.el7/dependencies/HEP_OSlibs.x86_64.dependencies-recursive-flat.txt.

qa_pairs.question: How many main repositories for HEP-related container images are currently known?
qa_pairs.answer: As of this writing, there are two main repositories for HEP-related container images, both distributed via CVMFS repositories.

qa_pairs.question: What are the two main repositories for HEP-related container images?
qa_pairs.answer: The two main repositories are from ATLAS and WLCG.

qa_pairs.question: Where can one find documentation for ATLAS distributions of Singularity images?
qa_pairs.answer: Documentation for ATLAS distributions of Singularity images is available at https://twiki.cern.ch/twiki/bin/view/AtlasComputing/ADCContainersDeployment.

qa_pairs.question: Where are the single file packed ATLAS Singularity images located?
qa_pairs.answer: Single file packed ATLAS Singularity images are located at `/cvmfs/atlas.cern.ch/repo/containers/images/singularity/`.

qa_pairs.question: Where are the unpacked ATLAS Singularity images located?
qa_pairs.answer: Unpacked ATLAS Singularity images are located at `/cvmfs/atlas.cern.ch/repo/containers/fs/singularity/`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the WLCG unpacked repository?
qa_pairs.answer: The WLCG unpacked repository is a development project that uses DUCC to automate publishing of container images from a Docker image registry to CVMFS.

qa_pairs.question: What tool does the WLCG unpacked repository use to automate publishing container images?
qa_pairs.answer: The WLCG unpacked repository uses DUCC to automate publishing container images.

qa_pairs.question: In what formats are container images published to CVMFS by the WLCG unpacked repository?
qa_pairs.answer: The images are published to CVMFS in a standard directory structure format for Singularity, and in the layered format used by Docker, allowing Docker to instantiate images directly from CVMFS using the graph driver plugin.

qa_pairs.question: Where can additional documentation for the WLCG unpacked repository project be found?
qa_pairs.answer: Additional documentation for this project is available at https://github.com/cvmfs/ducc.

qa_pairs.question: Where can one find the list of container images automatically published by the WLCG unpacked repository?
qa_pairs.answer: The list of automatically published images is at https://gitlab.cern.ch/unpacked/sync/blob/master/recipe.yaml.

qa_pairs.question: How can a user add an image to the list of automatically published images in the WLCG unpacked repository?
qa_pairs.answer: To add an image to the list, a user can submit a merge request.

qa_pairs.question: What is an example of an image included in the WLCG unpacked repository's automatically published list?
qa_pairs.answer: The `atlas-grid-centos7` image is included in the list of images automatically published by the WLCG unpacked repository.

qa_pairs.question: Where are the images from the WLCG unpacked repository located in CVMFS?
qa_pairs.answer: The images from the WLCG unpacked repository are located under `/cvmfs/unpacked.cern.ch/`.

qa_pairs.question: Why is the singularity executable installed outside the usual Compute Canada CVMFS stack?
qa_pairs.answer: The singularity executable is installed outside the usual Compute Canada CVMFS stack because it runs in a setuid environment by default.

qa_pairs.question: Which versions of Singularity are most likely available on Compute Canada sites as of this writing?
qa_pairs.answer: As of this writing, versions 2.6.1, 3.2.0, and 3.3.0 are the most likely candidates for Singularity on Compute Canada sites.

qa_pairs.question: What is the recommended practice when invoking Singularity on Compute Canada sites?
qa_pairs.answer: It is best to invoke the necessary version of Singularity, as various versions are available and defaults may change.

qa_pairs.question: What are the paths or commands to invoke Singularity on Cedar, Graham, and Niagara?
qa_pairs.answer: On Cedar and Graham, the path is `/opt/software/singularity-x.x.x`. On Niagara, you should `module load singularity` and then use `/opt/singularity/2`.

qa_pairs.question: What are two ways to invoke a container from a CVMFS repository?
qa_pairs.answer: A container from a CVMFS repository can be invoked either directly (as the image will be cached) or by pulling the image and storing it in your local area.

qa_pairs.question: Why might pulling a container image to your local area be beneficial when invoking it from CVMFS?
qa_pairs.answer: Pulling a container image to your local area may offer some performance benefits, depending on the system.
